listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Protozoan" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Protozoan: A single-celled eukaryotic microorganism
wrongAnswers: Culture: Microbes grown under controlled conditions
Gram stain: A staining method that helps classify bacteria
Host cell: A cell used by a virus or microbe to reproduce

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Microbiome" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Microbiome: The community of microorganisms living in an environment
wrongAnswers: Petri dish: A shallow dish used to grow microorganisms
Colony: A visible group of microbes growing from cells
Biofilm: A surface-attached community of microorganisms

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Pathogen" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Pathogen: A microbe or agent that can cause disease
wrongAnswers: Aseptic technique: Methods used to prevent unwanted contamination
Fermentation: Microbial metabolism that produces useful products without oxygen
Bacterium: A single-celled organism without a nucleus

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Antibiotic" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Antibiotic: A drug used to treat bacterial infections
wrongAnswers: Sterilization: Destroying or removing all living microbes
Yeast: A single-celled fungus often used in baking and brewing
Virus: An infectious particle that replicates inside host cells

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Antibiotic resistance" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Antibiotic resistance: The ability of bacteria to survive an antibiotic
wrongAnswers: Disinfectant: A chemical used to reduce microbes on surfaces
Capsid: The protein coat around a virus
Fungus: An organism group that includes yeasts and molds
